svg.text: TIGRINYA
HISTORY & HORN OF AFRICA
TIGRINYA ORIGINS
Tigrinya descended from Ge'ez (ancient Aksumite language)
Aksum Empire (~100-940 CE): one of ancient world's great powers
Aksum: first empire to mint gold coins in sub-Saharan Africa
~4th c. CE: Emperor Ezana converts to Christianity (first African empire)
Ge'ez: liturgical language of Ethiopian Orthodox Church; still used today
TIGRINYA TODAY
~8M speakers; official language of Eritrea (with Arabic)
Also spoken in Ethiopia's Tigray region (~4M more speakers)
2020-2022: Tigray War; devastating conflict in Tigrinya-speaking region
OROMO HISTORY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
OROMO ORIGINS
Oromo: indigenous to Ethiopian highlands and Great Rift Valley
Gadaa democratic system: age-grade governance predates modern democracy
16th c.: Oromo migration expanded across Ethiopian highlands
Menelik II (1889-1913): conquered Oromo lands for Amhara empire
OROMO SUPPRESSION & RESISTANCE
Amhara-dominated Ethiopia: Oromo culture and language marginalized
Oromo Liberation Front (OLF): founded 1973; decades of resistance
1991: Oromo gains recognition after Derg regime falls
Qubee Latin script: adopted as symbol of Oromo identity, not Amhara
OROMO TODAY
~40M speakers; largest ethnic group in Ethiopia
Oromia: largest regional state; surrounds Addis Ababa
Abiy Ahmed: Oromo-descended prime minister; Nobel Peace Prize 2019
SEMITIC + CUSHITIC: ONE HIGHLAND
Tigrinya (Semitic) and Oromo (Cushitic): neighboring peoples of Ethiopia
Both in Ethiopian highlands: shared geography, shared ecological pressures
Ethiopian Linguistic Area: languages converged across family lines
Both: Christian / Muslim communities within each language group
Addis Ababa: built between Tigrinya north and Oromo south
PLATE 34d ALT · TIGRINYA / OROMO · HISTORY
